similar_questions.title: Number of Connected Components in an Undirected Graph

discussion_posts.name: CivilCS
discussion_posts.content:
Tips:
- Check if there is a cycle
- Check if there the graph is one component

discussion_posts.name: RohitSgh
discussion_posts.content:
**Theorem** 
> A connected graph on $n$ nodes is a tree if and only if it has $n−1$ edges. 

For [Further Reading ](http://compalg.inf.elte.hu/~tony/Oktatas/TDK/FINAL/Chap%204.PDF). In other words
> Any connected graph with $n$ nodes and $n−1$ edges is a tree.

Thus, we should return `true` if **both** of these are `true`.
- Given `n`, number of `edges` should be `n-1`
- All `n` nodes should be connected. In other words, there should be only 1 [Connected Component](https://leetcode.com/problems/number-of-connected-components-in-an-undirected-graph/).

discussion_posts.name: namhlahade1
discussion_posts.content: Can we assume that if it is a tree, the root would be the zero node?

discussion_posts.name: user0252t
discussion_posts.content:
The common definition is a Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) where each node has only 1 parent.

But this question talks about an undirected graph, therefore is not a DAG (therefore not a tree).

Also, how can you even ask "how many parents does this node have" if the edges are not directed?

discussion_posts.name: Nintja
discussion_posts.content: Can anyone tell me why with one node and no edges the answer expected is true?\n\nedgeCount = 1, edges = [], expected: True

discussion_posts.name: cfabri6
discussion_posts.content:
the test is

4
[[2,3],[1,2],[1,3]]

The test currently wants a False - presumably b/c the number of nodes 4 is not equal to the number of nodes visited (1,2,3).

However, I see 3 valid nodes (1,2,3)...and 3 edges -  so, indeed there is a cycle since there is one too many edges and indeed the graph is connected - so per the vaguely implied criteria for 'validity', this graph is valid eg true.

the test should return True and not False and the node count should be 3, not 4.

discussion_posts.name: mohamedlaminef
discussion_posts.content: daily problem and premium weekly problems are graph theory questions that deal with undirected trees. They use identical edge list formats and require validating the same core tree properties (connectivity + no cycles).
